source: CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
title: Ensure ‘Log_min_error_statement’ Database Flag for Cloud SQL PostgreSQL Instance Is Set to ‘Error’ or Stricter

input_row.source: CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure ‘Log_min_error_statement’ Database Flag for Cloud SQL PostgreSQL Instance Is Set to ‘Error’ or Stricter
input_row.description: The log_min_error_statement flag defines the minimum message severity level that are considered as an error statement. Messages for error statements are logged with the SQL statement. Valid values include (from lowest to highest severity) DEBUG5, DEBUG4, DEBUG3, DEBUG2, DEBUG1, INFO, NOTICE, WARNING, ERROR, LOG, FATAL, and PANIC. Each severity level includes the subsequent levels mentioned above. Ensure a value of ERROR or stricter is set.
input_row.rationale: Auditing helps in troubleshooting operational problems and also permits forensic analysis. If log_min_error_statement is not set to the correct value, messages may not be classified as error messages appropriately. Considering general log messages as error messages would make is difficult to find actual errors and considering only stricter severity levels as error messages may skip actual errors to log their SQL statements. The log_min_error_statement flag should be set to ERROR or stricter. This recommendation is applicable to PostgreSQL database instances. Impact: Turning on logging will increase the required storage over time. Mismanaged logs may cause your storage costs to increase. Setting custom flags via command line on certain instances will cause all omitted flags to be reset to defaults. This may cause you to lose custom flags and could result in unforeseen complications or instance restarts. Because of this, it is recommended you apply these flags changes during a period of low usage.
input_row.audit: From Google Cloud Console 1. Go to the Cloud SQL Instances page in the Google Cloud Console by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/sql/instances. 2. Select the instance to open its Instance Overview page 3. Go to Configuration card 4. Under Database flags, check the value of log_min_error_statement flag is configured as to ERROR or stricter.  From Google Cloud CLI 1. Use the below command for every Cloud SQL PostgreSQL database instance to verify the value of log_min_error_statement is set to ERROR or stricter. gcloud sql instances describe <INSTANCE_NAME> --format=json | jq '.[].settings.databaseFlags[] | select(.name=="log_min_error_statement")|.value' In the output, database flags are listed under the settings as the collection databaseFlags.
input_row.remediation: From Google Cloud Console 1. Go to the Cloud SQL Instances page in the Google Cloud Console by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/sql/instances. 2. Select the PostgreSQL instance for which you want to enable the database flag. 3. Click Edit. 4. Scroll down to the Flags section. 5. To set a flag that has not been set on the instance before, click Add item, choose the flag log_min_error_statement from the drop-down menu and set appropriate value. 6. Click Save to save your changes. 7. Confirm your changes under Flags on the Overview page. From Google Cloud CLI 1. Configure the log_min_error_statement database flag for every Cloud SQL PosgreSQL database instance using the below command. gcloud sql instances patch <INSTANCE_NAME> --database-flags log_min_error_statement=<DEBUG5|DEBUG4|DEBUG3|DEBUG2|DEBUG1|INFO|NOTICE|WARNI NG|ERROR> Note : This command will overwrite all database flags previously set. To keep those and add new ones, include the values for all flags you want set on the instance; any flag not specifically included is set to its default value. For flags that do not take a value, specify the flag name followed by an equals sign ("="). Default Value: By default log_min_error_statement is ERROR. References: 1. https://cloud.google.com/sql/docs/postgres/flags 2. https://www.postgresql.org/docs/current/runtime-config-logging.html#GUC-LOG- MIN-ERROR-STATEMENT Additional Information: WARNING: This patch modifies database flag values, which may require your instance to be restarted. Check the list of supported flags - https://cloud.google.com/sql/docs/postgres/flags - to see if your instance will be restarted when this patch is submitted. Note : some database flag settings can affect instance availability or stability and remove the instance from the Cloud SQL SLA. For information about these flags, see Operational Guidelines. Note : Configuring the above flag does not require restarting the Cloud SQL instance.
input_row.impact: Turning on logging will increase the required storage over time. Mismanaged logs may cause your storage costs to increase. Setting custom flags via command line on certain instances will cause all omitted flags to be reset to defaults. This may cause you to lose custom flags and could result in unforeseen complications or instance restarts. Because of this, it is recommended you apply these flags changes during a period of low usage.
input_row.references: 1. https://cloud.google.com/sql/docs/postgres/flags 2. https://www.postgresql.org/docs/current/runtime-config-logging.html#GUC-LOG- MIN-ERROR-STATEMENT Additional Information: WARNING: This patch modifies database flag values, which may require your instance to be restarted. Check the list of supported flags - https://cloud.google.com/sql/docs/postgres/flags - to see if your instance will be restarted when this patch is submitted. Note : some database flag settings can affect instance availability or stability and remove the instance from the Cloud SQL SLA. For information about these flags, see Operational Guidelines. Note : Configuring the above flag does not require restarting the Cloud SQL instance.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
The audit for this control can be fully automated. The instructions provided include a Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) CLI command, indicating that it is possible to query the necessary configuration via GCP APIs. The control is about a standard GCP service property, specifically related to Cloud SQL database instance configuration, which is exposed via the GCP APIs.

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_sql_instance_log_min_error_statement_level_check

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Utilize the Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) command to verify the value of the `log_min_error_statement` database flag for Cloud SQL PostgreSQL instances.
- **GCP CLI Command:**
  - `gcloud sql instances describe <INSTANCE_NAME> --format=json | jq '.[].settings.databaseFlags[] | select(.name=="log_min_error_statement")|.value'`
- **Resources to Enumerate:**
  - All Cloud SQL PostgreSQL database instances within the GCP environment.
- **Configuration Property to Validate:**
  - Check that the `log_min_error_statement` flag is set to `ERROR` or stricter (e.g., `FATAL`, `PANIC`).
- **Pass/Fail Criteria:**
  - Pass: If the retrieved value is `ERROR`, `FATAL`, or `PANIC`.
  - Fail: If the value is set to anything less restrictive than `ERROR`.
- **Actionable Outcome:**
  - Use the `gcloud sql instances patch` command to remediate non-compliant instances, if needed, by setting the flag to a compliant value.

By automating this check, CSPM tools can continuously monitor Cloud SQL instances to ensure compliance with the CIS GCP Foundation Benchmark.
step2_review:
---

### 1. **APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE

- **Manual vs Automated:**  
  The classification as "Automated" is correct. The `log_min_error_statement` flag for Cloud SQL PostgreSQL instances is exposed via the GCP API and can be queried using the Google Cloud SDK (`gcloud`) CLI. No manual intervention is required to collect or validate this configuration.
- **GCP SDK Capabilities:**  
  The `gcloud sql instances describe` command retrieves all instance settings, including `databaseFlags`. This is fully supported by GCP APIs.
- **Other Automation Tools:**  
  While GCP Config does not natively support this specific flag, the approach using the SDK/CLI is valid. (CloudFormation is AWS-specific and not relevant.)

---

### 2. **TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 7/10

**Strengths:**
- The use of `gcloud sql instances describe <INSTANCE_NAME>` is correct for retrieving instance configuration.
- The logic to check for `log_min_error_statement` is sound.

**Issues:**
- **Incorrect jq Usage:**  
  The provided command:  
  ```bash
  gcloud sql instances describe <INSTANCE_NAME> --format=json | jq '.[].settings.databaseFlags[] | select(.name=="log_min_error_statement")|.value'
  ```
  is incorrect because the output of `gcloud sql instances describe` is a single JSON object, not an array. The correct jq filter should be:
  ```bash
  gcloud sql instances describe <INSTANCE_NAME> --format=json | jq '.settings.databaseFlags[] | select(.name=="log_min_error_statement") | .value'
  ```
- **Enumeration of Instances:**  
  The assessment does not specify how to enumerate all PostgreSQL instances. This is critical for automation at scale. The correct approach is:
  ```bash
  gcloud sql instances list --format="value(name,databaseVersion)"
  ```
  and then filter for `databaseVersion` starting with `POSTGRES`.
- **Program Name:**  
  The program name `gcp_sql_instance_log_min_error_statement_level_check` is descriptive but could be more precise. It should indicate "postgresql" and that this is a "database_flag" check.

---

### 3. **IMPROVEMENTS:**

- **Correct jq Filter:**  
  Update the jq filter as above to avoid errors.
- **Instance Enumeration:**  
  Add logic to enumerate only PostgreSQL instances:
  ```bash
  gcloud sql instances list --format="value(name,databaseVersion)" | grep POSTGRES | awk '{print $1}'
  ```
- **Consider API Alternative:**  
  For large environments, consider using the [Cloud SQL Admin API](https://cloud.google.com/sql/docs/mysql/admin-api/rest/v1beta4/instances) directly for bulk queries.
- **Program Name Suggestion:**  
  `gcp_sql_postgresql_instance_database_flag_log_min_error_statement_check`
- **Pass/Fail Logic:**  
  Explicitly document that values must be case-insensitive and only accept `ERROR`, `FATAL`, or `PANIC` (case-insensitive match).
- **Remediation Note:**  
  Remediation with `gcloud sql instances patch` may require a database restart for the flag to take effect. This operational impact should be noted.

---

### 4. **CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

- The control is well understood.
- The GCP APIs and SDK/CLI fully support this check.
- Minor technical corrections are needed, but the overall approach is sound.

---

### 5. **FINAL RECOMMENDATION:** MODIFY

**Summary of Required Modifications:**
- Correct the jq filter in the CLI command.
- Add explicit enumeration of PostgreSQL instances.
- Refine the program name for clarity and convention.
- Document case-insensitive value checks and operational impact of remediation.

**Example Improved Audit Steps:**
```bash
for INSTANCE in $(gcloud sql instances list --format="value(name,databaseVersion)" | grep POSTGRES | awk '{print $1}'); do
  VALUE=$(gcloud sql instances describe $INSTANCE --format=json | jq -r '.settings.databaseFlags[] | select(.name=="log_min_error_statement") | .value')
  if [[ ! "$VALUE" =~ ^(ERROR|FATAL|PANIC)$ ]]; then
    echo "$INSTANCE is NON-COMPLIANT: log_min_error_statement=$VALUE"
  fi
done
```

---

**Summary Table:**

| Section             | Score/Decision | Notes                                                                                                  |
|---------------------|---------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Approach Validation | AGREE          | Automated is correct                                                                                   |
| Technical Accuracy  | 7/10           | Minor CLI/jq errors, incomplete enumeration, program name could be improved                            |
| Improvements        | -             | See above: correct jq, enumerate Postgres only, better naming, note remediation impact                 |
| Confidence          | HIGH           | APIs/CLI fully support this check                                                                      |
| Recommendation      | MODIFY         | Fix CLI/jq, enumerate Postgres, clarify program name, document remediation impact                      |

---
